start: ^Senior year feels like forever away, and everything still smells like fresh notebooks, floor wax, and cafeteria pizza.
week_1_monday: ^The front doors of Jefferson High smell like floor wax, old lockers, and somebody's burnt toast Pop-Tarts.
^Alex jogs up beside you in a brand-new Linkin Park shirt, trying to act chill and failing.
^Alex: "Yo, real talk. This year gets random fast. We still rolling together, right? Dream team?"

c-0: ^You bump fists like it's a blood oath.
c-1: ^Alex nods, but his smile goes a little sketchy at the edges.

week_1_monday_after_alex_choice: ^Sarah swings through the front doors with the Popular crowd, sunlight catching a bracelet like a signal flare.
^Your pulse does that dumb fast-forward thing.
^Look down and pretend your backpack zipper is fascinating.

c-0: ^You throw a casual wave. She gives a quick half-smile before getting pulled into hallway traffic.
c-1: ^You suddenly become very interested in your shoes.

week_1_tuesday: ^Ms. Carter drops a stack of summer reading diagnostics on each desk.
^The room fills with the sound of paper sliding and quiet panic.
^Ms. Carter: "Pencils down in twenty. Try not to embarrass yourselves."
^Put your head down and eat the zero.

b: ^You reverse-engineer half the questions from context clues and pure audacity.
^You bubble in answers like a magic 8-ball with trust issues.

c-1: ^You go full slacker posture and let the clock do the work.

week_1_tuesday_after_diagnostic: ^After class, Lily shoulder-checks past your desk, clutching a color-coded folder.
^Lily: "Some of us actually did the assignment. Wild concept, I know."
^The rivalry file opens in your head with a loud click.

week_1_wednesday: ^The cafeteria looks like a map with invisible borders.
^Alex is at a safe middle table, Sarah is with Marcus and the Populars, and Derek is posted by the exit like he owns the fire lane.
^Sit with Alex at the safe table.
^Walk past them all and talk to Derek by the exit.

c-0: ^Alex immediately starts ranking every cafeteria pizza since sixth grade.
c-1: ^You land at the edge of their orbit, smiling like you meant to be there.
c-2: ^Derek smirks. "Nice. Most people freeze at the border."

week_1_wednesday_end: ^The bell hits before lunch feels finished.
week_1_thursday: ^The modem screams, then settles into that grainy dial-up hush.
^Yahoo! Messenger pops open. Your buddy list flickers online.
^Chloe: "today in gym was a total nightmare. i tripped during volleyball and everyone saw :("
^Support her. Stay in the chat and listen.
^Pivot: Ask what Sarah said about you.

c-0: ^You let her vent, crack a dumb joke, and she finally sends back "lol thanks."
c-1: ^You steer the convo toward Sarah. Chloe answers, but the typing pauses get longer.

week_1_thursday_away_message: ^Before logging off, you set your first away message:

c-2: ^Away Message: "if found, return to the void."

week_1_thursday_end: ^Your CRT hums in the dark while the status icon flips to away.
week_1_friday: ^You stand by the buses while everyone acts like they already have Friday night plans.

week_1_friday_derek: ^Derek appears out of nowhere, hoodie up.
^Derek: "Diner run. Real fries, real people. You in?"

c-0: ^You bail before anyone can call it a phase.

week_1_friday_marcus: ^Marcus palms a football and nods toward the parking lot.

c-0: ^You play it cool, even though your brain is buffering.
c-1: ^Marcus gives you a polite nod that still feels like a loss.

b: ^You start to answer, trip over your own timing, and the moment slips.

week_1_friday_lily: ^Lily hovers with a planner pressed to her chest like armor.
^Lily: "I'm hosting a study group Saturday. If you're serious about not tanking this year... show up."

c-0: ^Lily looks surprised, then immediately pretends she isn't.

week_1_reflection: ^The first week rewinds in your head while the buses pull out.
^Week 1 in the books. You are not invisible anymore.

week_2_sunday: ^Sunday night. Your desk is a mess of spiral notebooks, gel pens, and a printed elective form with your name misspelled.
^Outside, a car stereo leaks muffled Sum 41 through an open window down the block.
^This is the first real lock-in of freshman year.

c-3: ^You forge a parent signature to get out early.

week_2_monday: ^Ms. Carter slaps a packet on every desk like she's dealing cards in a casino you cannot afford.
^"First major group project. Fifteen percent of your grade. Due Friday after next."
^Someone groans. Someone else laughs too loudly. Nobody looks relaxed.
^Your pencil taps once against the desk before you can stop it.

b: ^You are running on fumes. The hallway lights feel too bright and your backpack suddenly weighs a hundred pounds.
^You head home before your brain blue-screens.

week_2_tuesday_hub: ^The final bell rings and the campus splits into cliques and excuses.
^Work on the project in the library (Cost: 1 Energy)

c-0: ^You camp between dusty encyclopedias and a humming CRT, pretending this is totally fine.
c-1: ^You trade jokes near the fountain and laugh at nothing, but everyone notices you were there.
c-2: ^You stare at the chain-link fence until your pulse drops back to normal.
c-3: ^You pack it in for the day.

week_2_wednesday: ^History class drags. Sarah drops a folded note near your desk without looking at you.
^Ms. Carter starts patrolling the aisles like a security camera with legs.

note_minigame: ^Ms. Carter's back is turned. The note is right there.

c-0: ^You catch a line about an upcoming party and two initials with a heart around them.

b: ^Ms. Carter turns around at exactly the wrong second.
^She snatches the note, drops it in the trash, and you feel every eye in the room.
^You catch a juicy rumor about Marcus before folding it shut again.

c-2: ^You play it safe and slide it under your notebook.

week_2_thursday: ^Dial-up screeches, then Yahoo! Messenger blinks alive.
^This week already feels heavier than Week 1.

c-0: ^Chloe: "freshman year is all stress and weird vibes. you okay?"
^You type: "yeah. mostly. just trying not to wipe out."
c-1: ^Alex: "project stuff is cursed. if i vanish, tell people i got eaten by homework."
^You laugh harder than the joke deserves.

week_2_friday: ^Friday hits like a sigh. You made it through:
^and at least one moment where your heart tried to launch out of your chest.
^your stats lean where you leaned, and people are starting to read you before you even say anything.
^The buses pull out under a washed-out sunset, and the week closes with your status still half-typed in your head.
week_3_monday: ^History class drags like it always does on a Monday.
^The clock clicks, someone taps a pencil, and the reading packet feels like it was written in another language.
^You slide Chloe a folded note while Ms. Carter writes dates on the board.

c-0: ^Chloe covers a laugh with her sleeve and writes back: "you're terrible."

week_3_monday_evening: ^That night, Alex pings you on Yahoo! Messenger about a custom cursor he downloaded.
^Alex: "It leaves a little comet trail, dude. This is the future."
^You type back like it's important, because right now it feels like just another normal night.

week_3_tuesday: ^The TV gets wheeled in on a metal cart.
^Volume low. No jokes. No side chatter.
^Marcus sits with his head down, wiping at his face.
^Derek stares out the window without blinking.
^Ms. Carter looks terrified and keeps gripping the remote like she might drop it.
^Today does not feel like a day you can spend.

c-0: ^You offer a tissue. The cliques don't matter today.
c-1: ^You just watch the screen, trying to process it.

week_3_tuesday_evening: ^That night, Yahoo! Messenger is a sea of away messages and short, uncertain statuses.
^You stay online later than usual, not really talking, just watching names glow on your buddy list.

week_3_wednesday: ^The cafeteria lines blur, table borders vanish, and nobody seems interested in the usual pecking order.
^At lunch, you see Sarah sitting alone outside on a bench, shoulders tight, eyes fixed on nothing.
^Sit with Sarah (No Rep check needed due to Unity)

c-0: ^You sit down. You don't even talk, just share the quiet.
c-1: ^You leave her alone. Sometimes quiet is better.

week_3_thursday: ^The modem squeals and settles into static hiss.
^You only have so much emotional fuel left tonight.

c-0: ^She wants to talk about the news. It's heavy, but necessary.
c-1: ^You run a dumb little flash game together and pretend, for ten minutes, that things are normal.

week_3_friday: ^Instead, everyone gathers on the field with candles and quiet voices.
^You stand on the grass and pick where to stand as the wind pushes smoke and wax into the night.

week_3_friday_end: ^The lights blur, the field fades, and the week closes in silence.

week_4_monday: ^Lockers clang shut in uneven rhythm while a Student Council table appears near the front office doors.
^There are flag ribbons, safety pins, and a handwritten sign about unity.
^Sarah catches your eye and holds one up.

c-0: ^You pin it to the zipper pull and nod like this is easy.
c-1: ^You keep walking. It feels performative to you.

week_4_tuesday: ^The PA crackles: random locker searches, effective immediately.
^Rows of students go quiet. Keys jingle. Sneakers squeak on waxed tile.
^At the locker next to yours, Derek goes pale.
^Derek: "Yo. Hold this for two minutes. Please."
^He tries to slide a Zippo and a crushed cigarette pack into your open backpack before admin reaches your row.
^Take the hit. Hide it for him.

c-0: ^You shove everything under your gym clothes. The admin passes. Your heart hammers anyway.
c-1: ^Derek curses, stuffs it back into his own bag, and gets pulled into the principal's office before last bell.

week_4_wednesday: ^Ms. Carter calls for "normal structure" and reassigns everyone to project mode.
^Lily is your partner and she is running on pure stress.
^She shoves a color-coded outline toward you.
^Lily: "If your margins are wrong, this whole thing looks amateur."

c-0: ^You tell her the outline doesn't matter right now. She glares like you kicked her planner.
c-1: ^You keep your head down and do the busywork line by line.

week_4_thursday: ^You get home ready to check Yahoo! Messenger, but the landline is busy.
^Your mom is on a long-distance call with relatives, pacing in the kitchen.
^By the time she hangs up, you're past your screen-time rules.

c-0: ^You slip online just long enough to send one message before your mom catches you.
c-1: ^You turn the monitor off and crash early.
^In the morning, Chloe's offline message is short and annoyed.

week_4_friday: ^The weekend arrives, but it doesn't feel lighter.
^You try to crack a joke near Sarah and Marcus to break the tension.
^It lands terribly. Nobody laughs. Someone coughs to fill the silence.
^By last period, the hallway map is back in place:
^Preps by the office, athletes near the gym, rebels by the side exit, everyone else orbiting the middle.
week_5_monday: ^Ms. Carter stands at the front of class with a red pen and no patience.
^Lily drops a giant outline on your desk that you definitely have not read.

c-0: ^You promise to format the whole thing tonight. Lily looks relieved, and you feel a headache starting.
c-1: ^You tell her to relax. She quietly adds your name to her slacker list.

week_5_tuesday: ^The library hums with giant CRT monitors and fluorescent lights.
^You're supposed to research sources, but Derek is hiding behind a bookshelf and motioning you over.

c-0: ^You ignore him and open AskJeeves instead. Derek rolls his eyes and disappears into the stacks.
c-1: ^You ditch the project to watch a banned flash animation. Lily sighs loudly from two tables over.

week_5_wednesday: ^During a dead-silent lecture, you try to pass Chloe a folded note about the project.
^Ms. Carter intercepts it mid-pass, taps it against her desk, and stares straight at you.

c-0: ^The paranoia follows you all day. Did she read it?
c-1: ^You play it off for the room, but Ms. Carter writes something on her clipboard.

week_5_thursday: ^You need to finish the project, but dial-up keeps dropping whenever someone grabs the phone.
^The clock keeps moving. Your energy does not.

c-0: ^You stay up until 2 AM and finish it. Your stomach feels like static.
c-1: ^You choose sanity over polish and accept a mediocre outcome.

b: ^Ms. Carter hands you a slip of paper. "Have your parents sign this."
^You're banned from Y!M for the weekend.
^Marcus nods in the hallway. "Not bad up there."

week_5_friday: ^September is over. The leaves are turning. Homecoming is right around the corner.
week_6_monday: ^A giant HOMECOMING banner hangs over the cafeteria doors like a warning label.
^Alex is already spiraling about pairings, rumors, and who asked who.
^Alex: "Okay, real question. What's your plan for the dance?"

c-0: ^You say it like you've had a plan all week. Alex exhales, relieved by your certainty.
c-1: ^You lean into the anti-establishment vibe and pretend none of this matters.
c-2: ^You shrug it off, but the pressure is officially on.

week_6_tuesday: ^You spot Chloe by her locker and almost walk past before your brain catches up.
^No oversized hoodie today. Her hair is different. Subtle, but not subtle.
^She looks up and gives you a nervous half-smile.

c-0: ^You stumble over the words. She blushes, looks away, and the air between you shifts.
c-1: ^You keep it casual. She smiles, but the moment stays in the just-friends lane.

week_6_wednesday: ^A Spirit Committee kid with a clipboard starts table-hopping, collecting dance answers.
^Then they stop right in front of you, with Sarah and Marcus close enough to hear everything.

c-0: ^You commit out loud. Sarah smiles, and suddenly this feels very real.
c-1: ^You clown on the clipboard routine. Derek laughs from down the hall.

week_6_thursday: ^You're set to Invisible on Y!M, pretending you're not watching status changes.
^Then Sarah updates to: "Listening to: Drops of Jupiter - Train."

b: ^You could just send one Buzz and stop overthinking this.
^Your fingers hover over the keyboard while your brain does laps.

c-0: ^You ping her and end up in a twenty-minute chat about songs, classes, and nothing.
c-1: ^You type "hey" and delete it five times, then log off in a classic cringe spiral.

week_6_friday: ^The whole school is packed into the bleachers while the band blasts through another pep tune.
^The principal wraps up announcements and hands off the mic.
^Marcus grabs it, walks to center court with a badly painted sign, and asks Sarah to Homecoming in front of everyone.
^Sarah looks overwhelmed, covers her face, and nods without saying yes into the microphone.
^You stay seated in the bleachers, watching your Phase 1 goal slide out of reach.
week_7_monday: ^Sarah telling Marcus she "needs to check with her parents" is all anyone can talk about, and everyone knows it's a stall tactic.
^Alex catches up with you by the lockers.

c-0: ^You tell Alex you're happy for Marcus. Safe play, high-status, no sudden explosions.
c-1: ^You start a rumor that Marcus only asked her on a dare.
c-2: ^You ignore the Marcus drama and ask Chloe what she knows. She says Sarah is overwhelmed by the public spectacle.

week_7_tuesday: ^Ms. Carter announces Marcus is on Academic Probation.
^If he fails Thursday's quiz, he's benched and banned from the dance.
^After class, Marcus corners you by the trophy case.

c-0: ^You spend the evening tutoring him on Y!M. Honorable friend mode.
c-1: ^You hand him a guide full of bad dates and half-truths. Your heart pounds the whole walk home.
c-2: ^You stay out of it. Not your circus, not your monkeys.

week_7_wednesday: ^You get a ten-minute gap between 3rd and 4th period.
^If there's a last clean chance to shoot your shot, this is it.

c-0: ^You walk up and ask her to go "as friends" before Marcus gets an answer.
^She looks shocked and says she'll think about it.
c-1: ^You fold the note tight and slide it through the locker vents.
^Lower risk, slower burn, no immediate answer.
c-2: ^You abandon the Sarah vortex, find Chloe, and ask.
^She says yes immediately. Relief hits like cold water.

b: ^You're sneaking online past house rules again.

week_7_thursday: ^"Marcus is freaking out about the quiz tomorrow."

c-0: ^You test the waters. She's Away, then replies with a smiley face a minute later.

b: ^You talk matching thrift-store outfits and shoes that won't kill your feet.
^She says she's probably going solo and shrugs it off with "lol whatever."

week_7_friday: ^After-school Pep Rally prep turns the hall into controlled chaos.

b: ^You watch the popular drama from the bleachers with Chloe and stop caring about who wins what.
^Marcus fails the quiz, screams in the hallway, and kicks a locker so hard it dents.
^Sarah tells him she isn't going with him.
^Sarah walks over, half-smiling: "So, about that note..."
^Marcus passes. He and Sarah lock in as the Power Couple.
^Sarah lets you down gently by the stairwell.

week_7_friday: ^The board is set. Next week is Spirit Week, and it all ends at the dance.
week_8_monday: ^Pajama Day turns the hallway into a flannel traffic jam.
^Oversized tees, plaid pants, slippers, and exactly one person in a bathrobe.
^You decide how hard you're participating in the school spirit machine.

c-0: ^You wore the most embarrassing plaid pants you own. It's a lot of visibility, but you own it.
c-1: ^You wear normal clothes and tape a paper badge to your chest: "This is my PJ." Derek nods in approval.
c-2: ^You hide in the library during lunch and wait for the spectacle to pass.

week_8_tuesday: ^No one planned ahead, so everyone is panic-matching in homeroom.

c-0: ^You both realize you're wearing the same gray hoodie. Alex acts like you won a championship.
c-1: ^You swap flannels to match. Her fingers linger when she hands your jacket back.
c-2: ^You match the Student Council colors. It works, even if you feel like a fraud.

week_8_wednesday: ^The whole school is in the gym and the noise is absurd.
^You get called to the floor for the Balloon Pop relay in front of what feels like everyone.

c-0: ^You sprint, dive, and pop the balloon at any cost. You win. Your knees pay the price.
c-1: ^You fail on purpose in the loudest, goofiest way possible. The crowd laughs with you, not at you.

week_8_thursday: ^Everyone is heading to the stadium under Friday-eve lights.
^"The game is for sheep. Meet me at the Water Tower. I snagged some Zima."

c-0: ^You freeze in the bleachers and clap when everyone else claps.
c-1: ^You sit on rusted metal watching stadium lights from far away, detached from the high school machine.

week_8_friday: ^The gym smells like floor wax and cheap cologne.
^A disco ball throws jagged light over the bleachers.

b: ^You're dancing with Sarah while Marcus glares from the punch bowl.
^You won the Phase 1 game, but the tension feels radioactive.
^She's wearing a dress you've never seen. She smells like vanilla.
^At some point you realize you don't care where Sarah is tonight.
^You lean by the bleachers with Derek and watch the drama like a movie.
^You lean by the bleachers with Alex and watch the drama like a movie.
^You feel untouchable, and a little lonely.
^You drift through the dance without a clear side, half in and half out.

week_8_friday: ^The song fades. The lights come up. The magic breaks, and it's just a sweaty gym again.
week_9_monday: ^The hallway is a minefield of whispers about the dance.

b: ^Marcus is glaring at you from across the lockers like he wants to start something.
^Chloe gives you a shy smile by the lockers, then immediately looks at the floor.
^People clock you, then look away fast, like they're still deciding what your story is.

week_9_monday: ^People are staring. You choose how to carry it.

c-1: ^You avoid the main hallway until the gossip cycle cools down.

week_9_tuesday: ^Ms. Carter finally hands back Week 5 project grades.

b:
^You got a D.

^She writes "See me" in hard red ink and taps the paper twice.
^You got a B-. Not great, not a disaster.

c-0: ^You try to talk your way into extra credit. She doesn't buy it.
c-1: ^You shrug and shove it into your backpack like it doesn't matter.
c-2: ^You fold the paper and move on with your day.

week_9_wednesday: ^You're passing Alex a folded note about a rumor.
^It slips and lands near the Popular table.

c-0: ^You practically tackle a chair to grab it first. Humiliating, but the secret survives.
c-1: ^You look away. They read it and laugh. Your face goes nuclear.

week_9_thursday: ^You're trapped in a chaotic three-way Y!M chat with Alex and Chloe.
^Alex is spamming Buzz because he's bored.
^Chloe keeps trying to talk about how weird everything feels after the dance.

c-0: ^You kill the noise and finally have a real conversation with Chloe.
c-1: ^You set Away and shut the laptop. You can't absorb one more emotion tonight.

week_9_friday: ^Outdoor hangouts are dying off, and winter extracurriculars are starting.
^You need a place to belong for the next stretch.

c-2: ^You choose nicotine and fries over school spirit.
^Your Freshman identity is no longer a draft. It's the final copy.

week_10_monday: ^A thin, official-looking envelope is waiting on your desk in Homeroom.
^Your name is printed in block letters, which somehow makes it worse.

b:
^The letter says you are failing.

^Your stomach drops.

^The report says "satisfactory."

^You exhale like you've been holding your breath all morning.

c-0: ^They're disappointed, but they appreciate the honesty.
^You're grounded from Y!M for two nights.
c-1: ^Your hand shakes, but the signature looks real enough.
^You bought yourself time, and a fresh layer of anxiety.
c-2: ^You tuck the letter into your folder before anyone asks about it.

week_10_tuesday: ^Everyone is arguing about Halloween plans between classes.
^Chloe catches you by your locker and asks if you want to do a duo costume.

b: ^She smiles, and for a second, it's not just a dorky costume.
^She high-fives you. The dream team is back.

c-1: ^You say you're going as something detached and edgy.

week_10_wednesday: ^Two invitations are on the table, and both feel like a test.

c-0: ^You see Sarah there. It's boring, but you don't get in trouble.
c-1: ^You're standing by a fire in the cold, checking your watch for curfew every five minutes.
c-2: ^You're too old for this and everyone knows it, but free candy beats dignity.

week_10_thursday: ^Everyone in first period looks like a zombie.
^The room smells like candy corn and cheap face paint.
^Ms. Carter is in a terrible mood and drops a surprise pop quiz on the class.

c-0: ^You force your eyes to stay open and grind through it.
c-1: ^You fake alertness and absorb almost nothing.

week_10_friday: ^Someone brings a 2.0-megapixel digital camera to school and starts a hallway photo circle.
^Then your Halloween photo appears, and you look absolutely ridiculous.

c-0: ^You lean into the joke. People warm up to you for it.
c-1: ^You try to explain it away. It only gets weirder.
^The temperature is dropping, and the real academic work is about to begin.

week_11_monday: ^It's freezing. The radiator clanks like it wants out of the wall.
^Lily is handing out color-coded study guides before first period.
^She stops at your desk and offers one without smiling.

c-0: ^You take it. It feels heavy, like an admission of defeat.
c-1: ^You decline with a smirk. Lily looks at you like you're already dead.

week_11_tuesday: ^You only have so much energy left.

c-0: ^You spend hours under fluorescent lights. You're exhausted, but the math starts making sense.
c-1: ^Derek says Midterms are a social construct. You feel cooler, but the panic in your gut gets louder.
c-2: ^You play it safe. No gains, but you feel rested.

week_11_wednesday: ^Alex leans in and whispers that someone stole a copy of the Math midterm.
^He needs a lookout to distract the janitor while he gets a copy.

b: ^You get caught. The janitor wasn't fooled.
^You pull it off. Alex hands you a crumpled sheet of answers.
^You're a hero in the back row.

c-1: ^You stay in your seat. Alex thinks you're lame, but you're not in the principal's office.

week_11_thursday: ^You're trying to research for History, but your mom is on the phone again.
^The burnout meter is no longer theoretical.

c-0: ^You get into a shouting match over the landline.
^You squeeze in 20 minutes of research, and the whole house feels tense.
c-1: ^You crash early and wake up Friday with an "Unfinished Work" penalty hanging over you.

week_11_friday: ^Ms. Carter pulls you aside after class.
^"You're smart, but your homework average is killing you. If you don't ace the Midterm, I have to call your parents."
^The weekend isn't a break - it's a countdown to Monday morning.
